root_text.an10.100:1.1: “Dasayime, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an10.100:1.3: Rāgaṁ, dosaṁ, mohaṁ, kodhaṁ, upanāhaṁ, makkhaṁ, paḷāsaṁ, issaṁ, macchariyaṁ, mānaṁ—
root_text.an10.100:1.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, dasa dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an10.100:2.1: Dasayime, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an10.100:2.3: Rāgaṁ, dosaṁ, mohaṁ, kodhaṁ, upanāhaṁ, makkhaṁ, paḷāsaṁ, issaṁ, macchariyaṁ, mānaṁ—
root_text.an10.100:2.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, dasa dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an10.100:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up ten things you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an10.100:1.3: Greed, hate, delusion, anger, acrimony, disdain, contempt, jealousy, stinginess, and conceit.
translation_text.an10.100:1.4: Without giving up these ten things you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an10.100:2.1: After giving up ten things you can realize perfection.
translation_text.an10.100:2.3: Greed, hate, delusion, anger, acrimony, disdain, contempt, jealousy, stinginess, and conceit.
translation_text.an10.100:2.4: After giving up these ten things you can realize perfection.”
